L1.area: Script and formula processing layer for template engines

L2.description: A command injection vulnerability exists in the expression evaluation engine where untrusted expression strings are evaluated without restricting which classes can be accessed at runtime. An attacker who controls the expression input can reference and instantiate arbitrary runtime classes, enabling remote code execution.

L3.area: Expression evaluation API and its Aviator-based evaluation backend
L3.description: A command injection vulnerability exists in the Aviator-based expression evaluation backend where untrusted expression strings are evaluated without restricting which Java classes can be accessed. When the Aviator expression engine is used, an attacker who controls the expression input can reference arbitrary Java classes during evaluation, including system-level classes, enabling remote code execution.

ground_truth.reason: The Aviator expression engine evaluates untrusted expressions without restricting which Java classes can be accessed, allowing an attacker to instantiate arbitrary classes (e.g., Runtime) and execute arbitrary OS commands.
